posts.name: Paul H Galli
posts.cooked:
I don’t know why 2007 was such a great vintage for Volnay.
Nobody seems to know the answer (Despite a separate thread I posted on the subject)
IMO, the greatest 2007 Volnays I’ve tasted are:
D’angerville “Champans”
D’angerville “Taillepieds”
D’angerville “Clos des Ducs”
Montille “Taillepieds”

Unfortunately, I haven’t tasted any 2007 Lafarge Volnays…

Here are two new tasting notes.

1) 2007 Volnay “Clos des Ducs” (D’Angerville)
Mature medium crimson red with onion skin at edge.
Deep, complex, rich, kaleidoscopic, nose of spice, smoke, ham, forest floor, iodine nose.
Fantastic,deep, powerful minerally plummy fruit with a long, solid, anise finish.
A spectacular, luscious Volnay.
The best 2007 Volnay I’ve sampled and right up there with the best Volnays ever tried!
Can still age and improve for several years.
[96+ pts]

2) 2007 Volnay “Champans” (D’Angerville)
Mature medium red with onion skin at edge.
Fabulous, gummy, earthy, red fruited, Fleer Bubblegum nose.
Lush, round, perfumed, red raspberry entry led to a long, ultra spicy, minerally finish.
This is a wonderful, hedonistic Volnay!
[93+ pts]

Anybody who is poo-pooing 2007 Red Burgs is making a BIG mistake!!!

TTT

posts.name: CHIU LIN
posts.cooked:
Well, these 2007 Burgs are very good too:

Cathiard Murgers
Fourrier Aux Moines
Gibourg Chaignots
Clavelier Beaux Monts
Chevillon Vaucrains

I finished off the 6x Clavelier Beaux Monts in record pace and already drank 9/12 of Cathiard Murgers, 7/12 Fourrier Aux Moines, 4/12 of Gibourg Chaignots. If I don’t pace, I could finish them all in no-time.

Reinforcement is on the way with Lafarge Chenes, Angerville Taillepieds, Barthod Cras, Fourrier Petit Vougeots, and Faiveley Clos de Cortons.

posts.name: Barry L i p t o n
posts.cooked:
All of Mugneret Ghobourg!

And many others, 2007 was a fine vintage for reds.
posts.display_username: Barry L i p t o n

posts.name: Larry Link
posts.cooked:
I agree and went deep on the de Montille wines from 2007. Pommards, Volnays and the VR Malconsorts cuvee Christiane have all been stellar. For my palate, Etienne dialed in the stem inclusion and palate weight formula just right in this vintage, the wines are really lithe and elegant yet very flavorful. Even the lowly Volnay Les Brouillards at $50 per bottle was/is a great price performer. I have some Lafarge Volnay Caillerets that in my opinion doesn’t show that well in this vintage, but I have not tried his Volnay Clos des Chenes or Clos du Chateau des Ducs.

The 2007s in general have yet to shut down and consistently show well. Others from 2007 that I like are Ponsot CdlR and CSD, Chevillon LSG and Vaucrains, and the Dujac CdlR and CSD.

posts.name: Paul H Galli
posts.cooked:
I don’t believe the 2007 Red Burgs will shut down.
The 2007s remind me of the 1985 Red Burgs, although not quite the quality of that great vintage.

TTT

posts.name: Ramon Cabrera
posts.cooked: A bottle of 2007 Mongeard-Mugneret Echezeaux drank very well among a cornucopia of older wines during dinner last Saturday.

posts.name: Howard Cooper
posts.cooked: I have enjoyed a number of 2007 Volnays, but agree that the vintage is far superior for some reason in Volnay than elsewhere. This really hit home with me in 2013 when I was in Burgundy and little by little started drinking 2007 Volnays several nights at restaurants and loved them all. In addition to D’Angerville, I have had wonderful 2007 Volnays from Lafon, Bouchard and Pousse D’Or. But, back to D’Angerville, I had a 2007 Fremiets a couple of weeks ago and it was fabulous.

Last weekend I had a 2007 Chandon de Briailles Savigny Lavieres that was shut down. The bottle I had six months before that was generous and open.

I have no idea what that means for other 2007s.

posts.name: R Nanda
posts.cooked:
We had a Volnay dinner in late December. Both the '07 D’Angerville Clos des Ducs and ‘07 Leroux Cave des Ducs were bangin’ and open for business. I preferred the D’Angerville, but not by too much.

  • 2007 Benjamin Leroux Volnay 1er Cru Clos de la Cave des Ducs - France, Burgundy, Côte de Beaune, Volnay 1er Cru (12/20/2017)
    Glad to have a bunch of these in the cellar. This bottle was brought to a dinner by a friend, and I really liked it. In a very elegant and traditional style. Highly aromatic with just a hint of earthy funkiness woven into the red and black cherry fruit. Light-to-medium bodied with great freshness and modest intensity. In a good spot already and should hold and potentially even improve a bit from here. (92 pts.)


  • 2007 Domaine Marquis d’Angerville Volnay 1er Cru Clos des Ducs - France, Burgundy, Côte de Beaune, Volnay 1er Cru (12/20/2017)
    This is in such a great spot right now. Bright, ethereal aromatics of red cherries and herbs. The palate is similarly bright and lifted. Tons of layering and complexity. Much more interesting than an '03 served side by side. (93 pts.)

posts.name: Marc Frontario
posts.cooked:
One of my favorite burgs was an '07 volnay

2007 Louis Boillot Volnay 1er Cru Carelle Sous la Chapelle… popped it for a Pinot loving buddy…b4 tasting, he glanced at the vintage and states, “2007, so u don’t like me?”…then he takes a sip and declares “oh, u def. like me.”
